architecture.key_insight: Registration is COMPLETELY separated from logic. reg/ package contains ALL ModXxx.java registry classes. common/ contains ALL game logic organized by feature (block/, items/, entities/, events/, network/, fluids/, worldgen/).

packages.reg: ALL registrations: ModRegistry, ModBlocks, ModItems, ModEntities, ModFluids, ModCreativeTabs, ModSounds, ModParticles, ModTags, ModRecipes, ModEnchantments, ModCommands, ModWorldgen, ModComponents, ModDamageSources, ModMenuTypes, ModConstants, ModSetup, ModTextures, ModSoftFluids, LootTablesInjects, ClientRegistry — 22 registry classes
packages.common: ALL game logic by feature: block/, items/, entities/, events/, network/, fluids/, worldgen/, commands/, inventories/, misc/, utils/

registration.strategy: complete separation of registry and logic
registration.pattern: reg/ package is a PURE DATA layer — it only registers things, no game logic. common/ package is PURE LOGIC — it references registrations from reg/ but never calls register(). This separation is extremely clean.

key_patterns.name: Registry-Logic Separation
key_patterns.description: The cleanest separation in MC modding: reg/ has zero game logic, common/ has zero registry calls. This is better than Farmer's Delight's registry/ pattern because the registry package is purely declarative.
key_patterns.benefit: You can change registration API without touching any game logic, and vice versa.

key_patterns.name: Multi-Loader Architecture
key_patterns.description: Supplementaries supports both Fabric AND Forge from the same codebase. The common/ package is shared. Platform-specific code is minimal and abstracted.
key_patterns.approach: common/ for shared logic, fabric/ and forge/ subprojects for loader-specific glue
